description: Environment template that pairs the nerium-integration-engineer agent definition with a scoped GitHub credential and a session-scoped filesystem prefix. Each MA session derived from this environment receives an isolated sandbox container and a fresh vault-materialised token; the plaintext token value never reaches the NERIUM backend.

vault_secret_refs_notes.github_token_scoped: Fine-grained GitHub PAT. Scope: contents:read, pull_requests:write, and optionally workflows:read on the single target repo. Never code repos outside that list. Rotation cadence: every 30 days manually during hackathon, automated via GitHub App installation token post-hackathon per ADR-004.

file_scope_prefix_notes: All artifacts written by the MA session under /mnt/session/outputs/ are surfaced through the Files API with scope_id = session_id. The file_scope_prefix is applied by the MaFilesApiClient as an assertion target for pullArtifacts; paths outside this prefix are logged but still returned.
vault_setup_instructions: Step 1. In GitHub, create a fine-grained Personal Access Token scoped to the single NERIUM integration target repo (Finerium/nerium for the hackathon demo). Permissions: Contents:read, Pull requests:read and write, Workflows:read, Metadata:read.

Step 2. In the Anthropic Console under Managed Agents > Vault, create a secret at path nerium/github-pat-scoped with the PAT value. Alternatively use the ant CLI: `ant vault secrets set nerium/github-pat-scoped --value <pat>`. The plaintext value never transits the NERIUM backend; it is held server-side.

Step 3. Register this environment template via `ant environments create --file ma_environment.nerium_integration_engineer.json` or the Console Environments UI. Confirm the vault reference resolves before spawning the first session.

Step 4. Day 1 smoke test: spawn one session with a trivial task prompt (ask the agent to print the GitHub API user response). Assert the Console trace shows a successful api.github.com call. If blocked, check the networking allow-list and vault ref resolution.

Step 5. Rotation: set a monthly reminder to re-issue the PAT and rewrite the vault secret. Sessions pick up the new value on next spawn; in-flight sessions continue with the value they were spawned with.
post_hackathon_refactor_notes: Replace PAT with a GitHub App installation token vended per tenant; update vault path schema to nerium/{tenant}/github-app-token.
Expand session_defaults.task_budget dynamically based on NERIUM Builder strategy (Opus all vs Collaborative Anthropic vs Auto); currently a static pair.
Add a separate environment template for non-GitHub integration targets (GitLab, Bitbucket) once multi-provider support lands.
